.json()\n262\t            .flatten_event(true)\n263\t            .with_target(true)\n264\t            .with_current_span(true)\n265\t            .with_span_list(false);\n266\t        // Apply OTel .with_target(true)\n277\t            .with_current_span(true)\n278\t            .with_span_list(false);\n279\t        registry()\n280\t            .with(filter)\n281\t            .with(json_layer)\n282\t            .init();\n283\t    }\n284\t\n285\t    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n286\t    // This is done via a separate info span %pod_id).entered();\n289\t\n290\t    info!(\n291\t        shards = config.shards,\n292\t        replication_factor = config.replication_factor,\n293\t        replica_groups = info!(\n328\t                pod_id = %pod_id,\n329\t                \"rebalancer worker task starting\"\n330\t            );\n331\t            // Load any persisted rebalance jobs from previous worker.run().await;\n336\t            error!(\"rebalancer worker task exited unexpectedly\");\n337\t        });\n338\t    } else {\n339\t        info!(\"rebalancer worker not available (no task store configured)\");\n340\t    }\n341\t\n342\t    // Start scoped key rotation background task (requires Redis)\n343\t    if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n344\t        let rotation_state = subscriber (plan §9).\n354\t        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n355\t        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n356\t        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n357\t        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n358\t        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n359\t        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n360\t        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n361\t        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n362\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n363\t            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n364\t            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n365\t                &redis_url,\n366\t                &key_prefix,\n367\t                move |session_id: String| {\n368\t                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n369\t                    revoked_total.inc();\n370\t                },\n371\t            )\n372\t            .await\n373\t            {\n374\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n375\t            }\n376\t        });\n377\t    }\n378\t\n379\t    // Load aliases from task store on startup (plan §13.7)\n380\t    // Aliases must be loaded before any request routing to ensure consistent resolution\n381\t    if let Some(ref task_store) = state.admin.task_store {\n382\t        let alias_registry = state.admin.alias_registry.clone();\n383\t        let store = task_store.clone();\n384\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n385\t            info!(\"loading aliases from task store\");\n386\t            match alias_registry.sync_from_store(&*store).await {\n387\t                Ok(()) => {\n388\t                    let count = alias_registry.list().await.len();\n389\t                    info!(count, \"aliases loaded successfully\");\n390\t                }\n391\t                Err(e) => {\n392\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to load aliases from task store\");\n393\t                }\n394\t            }\n395\t        });\n396\t    } else {\n397\t        info!(\"alias loading skipped (no task store configured)\");\n398\t    }\n399\t\n400\t    // Start drift reconciler background task (plan §13.5)\n401\t    // Uses the drift_reconciler from AppState which is already configured\n402\t    if let Some(ref drift_reconciler) = state.admin.drift_reconciler {\n403\t        let drift_reconciler = drift_reconciler.clone();\n404\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n405\t            info!(\"drift reconciler started\");\n406\t            drift_reconciler.run().await;\n407\t            error!(\"drift reconciler exited unexpectedly\");\n408\t        });\n409\t    } else {\n410\t        info!(\"drift reconciler not available (no task store configured)\");\n411\t    }\n412\t\n413\t    // Start anti-entropy worker background task (plan §13.8)\n414\t    // Uses the anti_entropy_worker from AppState which is already configured\n415\t    if let Some(ref anti_entropy_worker) = state.admin.anti_entropy_worker {\n416\t        let anti_entropy_worker = anti_entropy_worker.clone();\n417\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n418\t            info!(\"anti-entropy worker started\");\n419\t            anti_entropy_worker.run().await;\n420\t            error!(\"anti-entropy worker exited unexpectedly\");\n421\t        });\n422\t    } else {\n423\t        info!(\"anti-entropy worker not available (disabled or no task store configured)\");\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    // Start Mode C worker background task (plan §14.5 Mode C)\n427\t    // Processes chunked background jobs (dump import, reshard backfill)\n428\t    if let Some(ref mode_c_worker) = state.admin.mode_c_worker {\n429\t        let mode_c_worker = mode_c_worker.clone();\n430\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n431\t            info!(\"Mode C worker started\");\n432\t            match mode_c_worker.run().await {\n433\t                Ok(()) => {\n434\t                    info!(\"Mode C worker exited cleanly\");\n435\t                }\n436\t                Err(e) => {\n437\t                    error!(\"Mode C worker exited unexpectedly: {}\", e);\n438\t                }\n439\t            }\n440\t        });\n441\t    } else {\n442\t        info!(\"Mode C worker not available (no task store configured)\");\n443\t    }\n444\t\n445\t    // Start peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5)\n446\t    // Periodically performs SRV lookups to discover peer pods\n447\t    if let Some(ref peer_discovery) = state.peer_discovery {\n448\t        let peer_discovery = peer_discovery.clone();\n449\t        let metrics = state.metrics.clone();\n450\t        let refresh_interval_s = config.peer_discovery.refresh_interval_s;\n451\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n452\t            let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(refresh_interval_s));\n453\t            info!(\n454\t                interval_s = refresh_interval_s,\n455\t                \"peer discovery refresh loop started\"\n456\t            );\n457\t            loop {\n458\t                interval.tick().await;\n459\t                match peer_discovery.refresh().await {\n460\t                    Ok(peer_set) => {\n461\t                        let count = peer_set.len() as u64;\n462\t                        info!(\n463\t                            peer_count = count,\n464\t                            \"peer discovery refresh completed\"\n465\t                        );\n466\t                        metrics.set_peer_pod_count(count);\n467\t                    }\n468\t                    Err(e) => {\n469\t                        error!(error = %e, \"peer discovery refresh failed\");\n470\t                    }\n471\t                }\n472\t            }\n473\t        });\n474\t    } else {\n475\t        info!(\"peer discovery disabled (not running in Kubernetes)\");\n476\t    }\n477\t\n478\t    // Start task registry TTL pruner background task (plan §4, Phase 3)\n479\t    // Runs on single-pod with advisory lock; Phase 6 §14.5 Mode A replaces with rendezvous\n480\t    if let Some(ref store) = state.admin.task_store {\n481\t        let store = store.clone();\n482\t        let pruner_config = config.task_registry.clone();\n483\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n484\t            // The pruner runs in its own thread via spawn_pruner\n485\t            let _pruner_handle = task_pruner::spawn_pruner(store, pruner_config);\n486\t            // The handle is dropped here only on process exit\n487\t            info!(\"task registry TTL pruner started\");\n488\t            // Keep this task alive forever\n489\t            std::future::pending::<()>().await;\n490\t        });\n491\t    } else {\n492\t        info!(\"task registry TTL pruner not available (no task store)\");\n493\t    }\n494\t\n495\t    // Start canary runner background task (plan §13.18)\n496\t    // Only enabled when canary_runner.enabled = true and Redis is available\n497\t    if config.canary_runner.enabled {\n498\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n499\t            let store: Arc<dyn TaskStore> = Arc::from(redis.clone());\n500\t            let canary_config = config.canary_runner.clone();\n501\t\n502\t            // Clone config values for the search executor\n503\t            let search_config = config.clone();\n504\t            let search_executor: miroir_core::canary::SearchExecutor = Arc::new(\n505\t                move |index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery| -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = miroir_core::error::Result<SearchResponse>> + Send>> {\n506\t                    let index_uid = index_uid.to_string();\n507\t                    let query = query.clone();\n508\t                    let config = search_config.clone();\n509\t\n510\t                    Box::pin(async move {\n511\t                        // For canary queries, we execute against the first available healthy node\n512\t                        let node_addresses: Vec<_> = config.nodes.iter()\n513\t                            .map(|n| n.address.clone())\n514\t                            .collect();\n515\t\n516\t                        for address in node_addresses {\n517\t                            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n518\t                                .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_millis(config.scatter.node_timeout_ms))\n519\t                                .build()\n520\t                            {\n521\t                                Ok(c) => c,\n522\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n523\t                            };\n524\t\n525\t                            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n526\t\n527\t                            // Build the search request body\n528\t                            let mut body = match serde_json::to_value(&query) {\n529\t                                Ok(v) => v,\n530\t                                Err(e) => return Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e))),\n531\t                            };\n532\t\n533\t                            // Add limit to avoid large responses for canary queries\n534\t                            if !body.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).is_some() {\n535\t                                body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(20);\n536\t                            }\n537\t\n538\t                            let response = match client.post(&url)\n539\t                                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", config.node_master_key))\n540\t                                .json(&body)\n541\t                                .send()\n542\t                                .await\n543\t                            {\n544\t                                Ok(r) => r,\n545\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n546\t                            };\n547\t\n548\t                            if response.status().is_success() {\n549\t                                if let Ok(text) = response.text().await {\n550\t                                    if let Ok(search_response) = serde_json::from_str::<SearchResponse>(&text) {\n551\t                                        return Ok(search_response);\n552\t                                    }\n553\t                                }\n554\t                            }\n555\t                        }\n556\t\n557\t                        // All nodes failed\n558\t                        Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::Topology(\n559\t                            \"All nodes failed for canary query\".to_string()\n560\t                        ))\n561\t                    })\n562\t                }\n563\t            );\n564\t\n565\t            // Create metrics emitter callback\n566\t            let metrics_for_canary = state.metrics.clone();\n567\t            let metrics_emitter: miroir_core::canary::MetricsEmitter = Arc::new(\n568\t                move |result| {\n569\t                    use miroir_core::canary::CanaryStatus;\n570\t                    let result_str = match result.status {\n571\t                        CanaryStatus::Passed => \"passed\",\n572\t                        CanaryStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n573\t                        CanaryStatus::Error => \"error\",\n574\t                    };\n575\t                    metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_runs(&result.canary_id, result_str);\n576\t                    metrics_for_canary.observe_canary_latency_ms(&result.canary_id, result.latency_ms as f64);\n577\t\n578\t                    for failure in &result.failed_assertions {\n579\t                        metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_assertion_failures(&result.canary_id, &failure.assertion_type);\n580\t                    }\n581\t                }\n582\t            );\n583\t\n584\t            // Create settings version checker callback\n585\t            let store_for_version = store.clone();\n586\t            let version_config = config.clone();\n587\t            let settings_version_checker: miroir_core::canary::SettingsVersionChecker = Arc::new(\n588\t                move |index_uid: &str| -> Option<i64> {\n589\t                    // Try to get the settings version from the task store\n590\t                    let node_ids: Vec<String> = version_config.nodes.iter()\n591\t                        .map(|n| n.id.clone())\n592\t                        .collect();\n593\t\n594\t                    let mut min_version: Option<i64> = None;\n595\t                    for node_id in node_ids {\n596\t                        if let Ok(Some(row)) = store_for_version.get_node_settings_version(index_uid, &node_id) {\n597\t                            match min_version {\n598\t                                None => min_version = Some(row.version),\n599\t                                Some(current) if row.version < current => min_version = Some(row.version),\n600\t                                _ => {}\n601\t                            }\n602\t                        }\n603\t                    }\n604\t                    min_version\n605\t                }\n606\t            );\n607\t\n608\t            // Create and start the canary runner\n609\t            let runner = CanaryRunner::new(\n610\t                store,\n611\t                canary_config.max_concurrent_canaries as usize,\n612\t                canary_config.run_history_per_canary as usize,\n613\t                search_executor,\n614\t                metrics_emitter,\n615\t                settings_version_checker,\n616\t            );\n617\t\n618\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n619\t                info!(\"canary runner started\");\n620\t                if let Err(e) = runner.start().await {\n621\t                    error!(\"canary runner exited: {}\", e);\n622\t                }\n623\t            });\n624\t        } else {\n625\t            info!(\"canary runner enabled but Redis not available - skipping\");\n626\t        }\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n630\t    let app = Router::new()\n631\t        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n632\t        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n633\t        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n634\t        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n635\t        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n636\t        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n637\t        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n638\t        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n639\t        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n640\t        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n641\t        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n642\t        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n643\t        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n644\t        // 2. auth_middleware\n645\t        // 3. Extension layers\n646\t        // 4. session_pinning_middleware - extracts X-Miroir-Session header\n647\t        // 5. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n648\t        // 6. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n649\t        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n650\t        //\n651\t        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n652\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n653\t            TelemetryState {\n654\t                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n655\t                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n656\t            },\n657\t            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n658\t        ))\n659\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n660\t            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n661\t        ))\n662\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n663\t            middleware::session_pinning_middleware,\n664\t        ))\n665\t        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n666\t            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n667\t        ))\n668\t        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n669\t        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n670\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n671\t            state.auth.clone(),\n672\t            auth::auth_middleware,\n673\t        ))\n674\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n675\t            auth::CsrfState {\n676\t                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n677\t                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n678\t            },\n679\t            auth::csrf_middleware,\n680\t        ))\n681\t        .with_state(state.clone());\n682\t\n683\t    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n684\t        .parse()\n685\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n686\t    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n687\t\n688\t    info!(\n689\t        main_addr = %main_addr,\n690\t        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n691\t        \"listening\"\n692\t    );\n693\t\n694\t    // Create listeners\n695\t    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n696\t    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n697\t\n698\t    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n699\t    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n700\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n701\t\n702\t    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n703\t    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n704\t    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n705\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n706\t\n707\t    // Run both servers concurrently\n708\t    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n709\t        main_server,\n710\t        metrics_server\n711\t    );\n712\t\n713\t    // Check for errors\n714\t    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n715\t        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n716\t    }\n717\t    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n718\t        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    Ok(())\n722\t}\n723\t\n724\t/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n725\t///\n726\t/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n727\t/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\n728\tasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n729\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n730\t        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n731\t    ));\n732\t\n733\t    loop {\n734\t        interval.tick().await;\n735\t\n736\t        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n737\t        let mut all_healthy = true;\n738\t\n739\t        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n740\t        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n741\t\n742\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n743\t            // Get current node status\n744\t            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n745\t\n746\t            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n747\t            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n748\t                continue;\n749\t            }\n750\t\n751\t            // Get node address\n752\t            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n753\t                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n754\t                None => {\n755\t                    all_healthy = false;\n756\t                    continue;\n757\t                }\n758\t            };\n759\t\n760\t            // Try to reach the node\n761\t            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n762\t                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n763\t                .build()\n764\t            {\n765\t                Ok(c) => c,\n766\t                Err(_) => {\n767\t                    all_healthy = false;\n768\t                    continue;\n769\t                }\n770\t            };\n771\t\n772\t            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n773\t            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n774\t\n775\t            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n776\t                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n777\t                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n778\t                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n779\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n780\t                }\n781\t            } else {\n782\t                all_healthy = false;\n783\t            }\n784\t        }\n785\t\n786\t        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n787\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n788\t            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n789\t            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n790\t        }\n791\t\n792\t        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n793\t        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n794\t\n795\t        // Update task registry size gauge\n796\t        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n797\t        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n798\t\n799\t        // Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus\n800\t        state.sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus().await;\n801\t\n802\t        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n803\t        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n804\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n805\t        }\n806\t\n807\t        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n808\t        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n809\t\n810\t        // Update §13.6 session pinning metrics\n811\t        state.session_manager.update_metrics(|count| {\n812\t            state.metrics.set_session_active_count(count as u64);\n813\t        });\n814\t\n815\t        // Prune expired sessions (plan §13.6)\n816\t        let pruned = state.session_manager.prune_expired().await;\n817\t        if pruned > 0 {\n818\t            info!(\n819\t                pruned_count = pruned,\n820\t                \"pruned expired sessions\"\n821\t            );\n822\t        }\n823\t    }\n824\t}\n825\t\n826\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n827\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n828\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n829\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n830\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n831\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n832\t\n833\t    // Per-node shard count\n834\t    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n835\t        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n836\t\n837\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n838\t        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n839\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n840\t            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n841\t                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n842\t            );\n843\t            for node_id in &assigned {\n844\t                let healthy = node_map\n845\t                    .get(node_id)\n846\t                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n847\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n848\t                if healthy {\n849\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n850\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n851\t                }\n852\t            }\n853\t        }\n854\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n855\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n856\t        } else {\n857\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n858\t        }\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n862\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n863\t    } else {\n864\t        1.0\n865\t    };\n866\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n867\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n868\t\n869\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n870\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n871\t    }\n872\t}\n873\t\n874\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n875\t///\n876\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n877\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n878\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n879\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n880\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n881\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n882\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n883\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n884\t\n885\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n886\t        if max > 0 {\n887\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n888\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n889\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n890\t        }\n891\t    }\n892\t\n893\t           .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n        registry()\n                  .build()\n                            {\n                                Ok(c) => c,\n                                Err(_) => continue,\n                            };\n\n                            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n\n                            // Build the search request body\n                            let responses for canary queries\n                            if !body.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).is_some() {\n                                body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(20);\n                            }\n\n                            let response = match client.post(&url)\n                                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", config.node_master_key))\n                                .json(&body)\n                                .send()\n                                .await\n                            {\n                                Ok(r) => r,\n                                Err(_) => continue,\n                            };\n\n                            if response.status().is_success() {\n                                if let Ok(text) = response.text().await {\n                                    if let Ok(search_response) = serde_json::from_str::<SearchResponse>(&text) {\n                                        return Ok(search_response);\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n\n                        // All nodes failed\n                        Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::Topology(\n                            \"All nodes failed for canary query\".to_string()\n                        ))\n                    })\n                }\n            );\n\n            // Create metrics emitter callback\n            let metrics_for_canary = state.metrics.clone();\n            let metrics_emitter: miroir_core::canary::MetricsEmitter = Arc::new(\n                move |result| {\n                    use miroir_core::canary::CanaryStatus;\n                    let result_str = match result.status {\n                        CanaryStatus::Passed => \"passed\",\n                        CanaryStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n                        CanaryStatus::Error => \"error\",\n                    };\n                    metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_runs(&result.canary_id, result_str);\n                    metrics_for_canary.observe_canary_latency_ms(&result.canary_id, result.latency_ms as f64);\n\n                    for failure in &result.failed_assertions {\n                        metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_assertion_failures(&result.canary_id, &failure.assertion_type);\n                    }\n                }\n            );\n\n            // Create settings version checker callback\n            let store_for_version = store.clone();\n            let version_config = config.clone();\n            let settings_version_checker: miroir_core::canary::SettingsVersionChecker = Arc::new(\n                move |index_uid: &str| -> Option<i64> {\n                    // Try to get the settings version from the task store\n                    let node_ids: Vec<String> = version_config.nodes.iter()\n                        .map(|n| n.id.clone())\n                        .collect();\n\n                    let mut min_version: Option<i64> = None;\n                    for node_id in node_ids {\n                        if let Ok(Some(row)) = store_for_version.get_node_settings_version(index_uid, &node_id) {\n                            match min_version {\n                                None => min_version = Some(row.version),\n                                Some(current) if row.version < current => min_version = Some(row.version),\n                                _ => {}\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    min_version\n                }\n            );\n\n            // Create and start the canary runner\n            let runner = CanaryRunner::new(\n                store,\n                canary_config.max_concurrent_canaries as usize,\n                canary_config.run_history_per_canary as usize,\n                search_executor,\n                metrics_emitter,\n                settings_version_checker,\n            );\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                info!(\"canary runner started\");\n                if let Err(e) = runner.start().await {\n                    error!(\"canary runner exited: {}\", e);\n                }\n            });\n        } else {\n            info!(\"canary runner enabled but Redis not available - skipping\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. session_pinning_middleware - extracts X-Miroir-Session header\n        // 5. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 6. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        //\n        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::session_pinning_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus\n        state.sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus().await;\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n\n        // Update §13.6 session pinning metrics\n        state.session_manager.update_metrics(|count| {\n            state.metrics.set_session_active_count(count as u64);\n        });\n\n        // Prune expired sessions (plan §13.6)\n        let pruned = state.session_manager.prune_expired().await;\n        if pruned > 0 {\n            info!(\n                pruned_count = pruned,\n                \"pruned expired sessions\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // Peer pod count is now set by peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5).\n    // Leader election is not yet implemented (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n    // Owned shards count will be set by Mode A rendezvous (plan §14.5).\n    metrics.set_leader(\"global\", true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":958,"startLine":1,"totalLines":958}}}

Phase 2 Integration Tests: Proxy + API Surface\n//!\n//! Tests the complete HTTP API surface with real Meilisearch nodes.\n//! Uses testcontainers for spinning up Meilisearch instances.\n\nuse miroir_core::config::{Config, NodeConfig};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, Topology};\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::sleep;\nuse testcontainers::{clients, ImageExt};\nuse testcontainers_modules::meilisearch::Meilisearch;\n\n/// Test configuration helper.\nstruct TestSetup {\n    meilisearch_urls: Vec<String>,\n    proxy_url: String,\n    master_key: String,\n    client: Client,\n}\n\nimpl TestSetup {\n    async fn new() -> anyhow::Result<Self> {\n        let docker = clients::Cli::default();\n\n        // Start 3 Meilisearch nodes\n        let mut meilisearch_urls = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..3 {\n            let meilisearch = Meilisearch::default()\n                .with_cmd_arg(format!(\"--master-key=key{}\", i))\n                .start(&docker)\n                .await?;\n\n            let port = meilisearch.get_host_port_ipv4(7700).await?;\n            let url = format!(\"http://localhost:{}\", port);\n            meilisearch_urls.push(url);\n        }\n\n        // Build topology config\n        let mut nodes = Vec::new();\n        for (i, url) in meilisearch_urls.iter().enumerate() {\n            nodes.push(NodeConfig {\n                id: format!(\"node-{}\", i),\n                address: url.clone(),\n                replica_group: (i % 2) as u32, // 2 replica groups\n            });\n        }\n\n        let config = Config {\n            shards: 16,\n            replication_factor: 2,\n            replica_groups: 2,\n            master_key: \"test_master_key\".to_string(),\n            admin: miroir_core::config::AdminConfig {\n                api_key: \"test_admin_key\".to_string(),\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            nodes,\n            server: miroir_core::config::ServerConfig {\n                bind: \"127.0.0.1\".to_string(),\n                port: 17770, // Non-standard port for testing\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        // Start the proxy in a separate task\n        let proxy_url = \"http://127.0.0.1:17770\";\n        // Note: In a real test, we'd spawn the proxy here\n        // For now, we'll assume it's already running\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            meilisearch_urls,\n            proxy_url: proxy_url.to_string(),\n            master_key: \"test_master_key\".to_string(),\n            client: Client::new(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Wait for the proxy to be ready.\n    async fn wait_for_ready(&self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        let deadline = tokio::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30);\n        while tokio::time::Instant::now() < deadline {\n            match self.client.get(&format!(\"{}/health\", self.proxy_url)).send().await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.status().is_success() => return Ok(()),\n                _ => sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await,\n            }\n        }\n        anyhow::bail!(\"Proxy did not become ready in time\")\n    }\n\n    /// Create an index.\n    async fn create_index(&self, uid: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        let body = json!({\n            \"uid\": uid,\n            \"primaryKey\": \"id\"\n        });\n\n        let resp = self.client\n            .post(&format!(\"{}/indexes\", self.proxy_url))\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !resp.status().is_success() {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"Failed to create index: {}\", resp.status());\n        }\n\n        // Wait for index to be created\n        self.wait_for_index(uid).await\n    }\n\n    /// Wait for an index to exist.\n    async fn wait_for_index(&self, uid: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        let deadline = tokio::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(10);\n        while tokio::time::Instant::now() < deadline {\n            match self.client\n                .get(&format!(\"{}/indexes/{}\", self.proxy_url, uid))\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.status().is_success() => return Ok(()),\n                _ => sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await,\n            }\n        }\n        anyhow::bail!(\"Index {} did not become ready\", uid)\n    }\n\n    /// Add documents to an index.\n    async fn add_documents(&self, uid: &str, documents: Vec<Value>) -> anyhow::Result<Value> {\n        let resp = self.client\n            .post(&format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents\", self.proxy_url, uid))\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&documents)\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !resp.status().is_success() {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"Failed to add documents: {}\", resp.status());\n        }\n\n        Ok(resp.json().await?)\n    }\n\n    /// Search an index.\n    async fn search(&self, uid: &str, query: &serde_json::Value) -> anyhow::Result<Value> {\n        let resp = self.client\n            .post(&format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", self.proxy_url, uid))\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(query)\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !resp.status().is_success() {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"Search failed: {}\", resp.status());\n        }\n\n        Ok(resp.json().await?)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a document by ID.\n    async fn get_document(&self, uid: &str, id: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Value> {\n        let resp = self.client\n            .get(&format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents/{}\", self.proxy_url, uid, id))\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !resp.status().is_success() {\n test_1000_documents_indexed_and_retrievable() {\n    let setup = TestSetup::new().await.expect(\"Failed to setup test\");\n    setup.wait_for_ready().await.expect(\"Proxy not ready\");\n\n    let index_uid = \"test_1000_docs\";\n\n    // Create index\n    setup.create_index(index_uid).await.expect(\"Failed to create index\");\n\n    // Generate 1000 documents\n    let documents: Vec<Value> = i),\n        }))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Add documents\n    let task = setup.add_documents(index_uid, documents).await.expect(\"Failed to add documents\");\n\n    // Wait for task to complete\n    .await\n        .expect(\"Failed to add documents\");\n\n    // Check for X-Miroir-Degraded header if any group was degraded\n    let degraded_header = resp.headers().get(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\");\n\n    // The write should succeed regardless\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success() || resp.status().as_u16() == 503);\n\n    // If degraded header is present, verify its format\n    if let Some(header) = degraded_header {\n        let header_value = header.to_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(header_value.starts_with(\"shards=\"), \"X-Miroir-Degraded should start with 'shards='\");\n    }\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore] // Requires docker\nasync fn test_error_format_parity_with_meilisearch() {\n    let setup = Invalid request (empty document batch)\n    let resp = setup.client\n        .post(&format!(\"{}/indexes/test/documents\", setup.proxy_url))\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", setup.master_key))\n        .json(&[])\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .expect(\"Request failed\");\n\n    assert_eq!(resp.status().as_u16(), 400);\n\n    let error: Value = resp.json().await.expect(\"Failed to parse error\");\n    assert!(error.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"Error should have 'message' field\");\n    assert!(error.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"Error should have 'code' field\");\n    assert!(error.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"Error should have 'type' field\");\n    assert!(error.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"Error should have 'link' field\");\n\n    // Verify error type is one of the known types\n    let error_type = error.get(\"type\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n    assert!([\"invalid_request\", \"auth\", \"internal\", \"system\"].contains(&error_type));\n\n    // 2. Not found (non-existent index)\n    let resp = setup.client\n        .get(&format!(\"{}/indexes/nonexistent\", setup.proxy_url))\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", setup.master_key))\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .expect(\"Request failed\");\n\n    assert!(resp.status().as_u16() == 404 || resp.status().as_u16() == 400);\n\n    // 3. Authentication error\n    let resp = setup.client\n        .get(&format!(\"{}/indexes/test\", setup.proxy_url))\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer invalid_key\")\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .expect(\"Request failed\");\n\n    assert!(resp.status().as_u16() == 401 || resp.status().as_u16() == 403);\n}\n","numLines":442,"startLine":1,"totalLines":442}}}

miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_error_shape_matches_meilisearch() {\n    // Test each MiroirCode variant\n    for code in MiroirCode::ALL {\n assert!(json.get(\"message\").unwrap().is_string(), \"'message' must be a string\");\n        assert!(json.get(\"code\").unwrap().is_string(), \"'code' must be a string\");\n        assert!(json.get(\"type\").unwrap().is_string(), \"'type' must be a string\");\n        assert!(json.get(\"link\").unwrap().is_string(), \"'link' must be a string\");\n\n        // Verify type is one \"system\"),\n            \"Error type must be one of: invalid_request, auth, internal, system\"\n        );\n\n        // Verify code has miroir_ prefix\n        let error_code = json.get(\"code\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            error_code.starts_with(\"miroir_\"),\n            \"Miroir error codes must have 'miroir_' prefix, got: {}\",\n            error_code\n        );\n\n        // Verify link format\n        let link = json.get(\"link\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            link.starts_with(\"https://github.com/jedarden/miroir\"),\n            \"Link must point to Miroir docs\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_http_status_codes() {\n    let test_cases = assert_eq!(code.error_type().to_string(), expected_type,\n            \"Error type for {:?} should be {}, got {:?}\",\n            code, expected_type, code.error_type());\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_serialization_is_deterministic() {\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, \"test message\");\n\n    // Serialize multiple times\n    let json1 = serde_json::to_string(&error).unwrap();\n    let json2 = serde_json::to_string(&error).unwrap();\n\n    // Should be byte-identical\n    assert_eq!(json1, json2, \"Error serialization must be deterministic\");\n\n    // Parse and verify structure\n    let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json1).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(parsed.get(\"message\").unwrap(), \"test message\");\n    assert_eq!(parsed.get(\"code\").unwrap(), \"miroir_no_quorum\");\n    assert_eq!(parsed.get(\"type\").unwrap(), \"system\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_meilisearch_error_parsing() {\n    // Test that we can parse forwarded Meilisearch errors\n    let meilisearch_error_json = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"Index not found\",\n        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n        \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#index_not_found\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = MeilisearchError::forwarded(meilisearch_error_json);\n\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Should successfully parse Meilisearch error\");\n    let error = parsed.unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(error.message, \"Index not found\");\n    assert_eq!(error.code, \"index_not_found\");\n    assert_eq!(error.error_type.to_string(), \"invalid_request\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_invalid_json_is_not_parsed_as_meilisearch_error() {\n    let invalid_json = r#\"{\"not\": \"an error\"}\"#;\n\n    let parsed = MeilisearchError::forwarded(invalid_json);\n\n    assert!(parsed.is_none(), \"Should not parse invalid JSON as Meilisearch error\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_code_roundtrip() {\n    // Test that code strings can be parsed back to MiroirCode\n    for code in MiroirCode::ALL {\n        let code_str = code.as_str();\n        let parsed = MiroirCode::from_code_str(code_str);\n\n        assert_eq!(parsed, Some(code), \"Code '{}' should roundtrip to {:?}\", code_str, code);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_unknown_code_returns_none() {\n    assert!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(\"unknown_code\").is_none());\n    assert!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(\"miroir_unknown\").is_none());\n    assert!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(\"\").is_none());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_primary_key_required_error() {\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired, \"primary key is required\");\n\n    let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"code\").unwrap(), \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"type\").unwrap(), \"invalid_request\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"message\").unwrap(), \"primary key is required\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_no_quorum_error() {\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, \"insufficient nodes available\");\n\n    let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"code\").unwrap(), \"miroir_no_quorum\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"type\").unwrap(), \"system\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"message\").unwrap(), \"insufficient nodes available\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_shard_unavailable_error() {\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"shard 5 is unavailable\");\n\n    let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"code\").unwrap(), \"miroir_shard_unavailable\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"type\").unwrap(), \"system\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_reserved_field_error() {\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"field '_miroir_internal' is reserved\"\n    );\n\n    let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"code\").unwrap(), \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"type\").unwrap(), \"invalid_request\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_timeout_error() {\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::Timeout, \"operation timed out\");\n\n    let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"code\").unwrap(), \"miroir_timeout\");\n    assert_eq!(json.get(\"type\").unwrap(), \"system\");\n    assert_eq!(error.http_status(), 504);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_message_preserves_content() {\n    let messages = vec![\n        \"simple message\",\n        \"message with: special chars\",\n        \"message with\\nnewlines\",\n        \"message with \\\"quotes\\\"\",\n        \"message with unicode: 🎉\",\n    ];\n\n    for msg in messages {\n        let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, msg);\n        assert_eq!(error.message, msg);\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json.get(\"message\").unwrap(), msg);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_all_miroir_codes_are_documented() {\n    // Verify all error codes have proper documentation links\n    for code in MiroirCode::ALL {\n        let error = MeilisearchError::new(code, \"test\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n        let link = json.get(\"link\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n\n        // Verify link contains the error code\n        assert!(\n            link.contains(&format!(\"#{}\", code.as_str())),\n            \"Documentation link for {:?} should reference the error code: {}\",\n            code, link\n        );\n\n        // Verify link is a valid URL\n        assert!(link.starts_with(\"https://\"));\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_response_includes_all_required_fields() {\n    // Create an error and verify it has all required fields\n    let error = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, \"test message\");\n\n    // Verify struct has all fields\n    assert!(!error.message.is_empty());\n    assert!(!error.code.is_empty());\n    assert!(error.link.is_some());\n\n    // Verify serialized JSON has all fields\n    let json = serde_json::to_value(&error).unwrap();\n\n    let obj = json.as_object().unwrap();\n    assert!(obj.contains_key(\"message\"));\n    assert!(obj.contains_key(\"code\"));\n    assert!(obj.contains_key(\"type\"));\n    assert!(obj.contains_key(\"link\"));\n\n    // Verify link is not null\n    assert!(obj.get(\"link\").unwrap().is_string());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_type_classification() {\n    // Test that MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n        MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n        MiroirCode::IndexAlreadyExists,\n    ];\n\n    for code in invalid_request_codes {\n        assert_eq!(code.error_type(), miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n        assert_eq!(code.error_type().to_string(), \"invalid_request\");\n    }\n\n    // auth errors\n    let auth_codes = vec![\n        MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n        MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n        MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n        MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n        MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n    ];\n\n    for code in auth_codes {\n        assert_eq!(code.error_type(), miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::Auth);\n        assert_eq!(code.error_type().to_string(), \"auth\");\n    }\n\n    // system errors\n    let system_codes = vec![\n        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n        MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n        MiroirCode::Timeout,\n    ];\n\n    for code in system_codes {\n        assert_eq!(code.error_type(), miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::System);\n        assert_eq!(code.error_type().to_string(), \"system\");\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_http_status_matches_error_type() {\n    // Verify HTTP status codes match error type conventions\n\n    // 4xx errors should be InvalidRequest or Auth\n    let client_error_codes = vec![\n        (MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired, 400),\n        (MiroirCode::ReservedField, 400),\n        (MiroirCode::JwtInvalid, 401),\n        (MiroirCode::InvalidAuth, 401),\n        (MiroirCode::MissingCsrf, 401),\n        (MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied, 403),\n        (MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch, 403),\n        (MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused, 409),\n        (MiroirCode::IndexAlreadyExists, 409),\n    ];\n\n    for (code, expected_status) in client_error_codes {\n        let status = code.http_status();\n        assert!(status >= 400 && status < 500,\n            \"Client error {:?} should have 4xx status, got {}\",\n            code, status);\n        assert_eq!(status, expected_status);\n    }\n\n    // 5xx errors should be System\n    let server_error_codes = vec![\n        (MiroirCode::NoQuorum, 503),\n        (MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, 503),\n        (MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale, 503),\n        (MiroirCode::Timeout, 504),\n    ];\n\n    for (code, expected_status) in server_error_codes {\n        let status = code.http_status();\n        assert!(status >= 500 && status < 600,\n            \"Server error {:?} should have 5xx status, got {}\",\n            code, status);\n        assert_eq!(status, expected_status);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":365,"startLine":1,"totalLines":365}}}

Meilisearch node error response body.\n172\t    ///\n173\t    /// Returns `None` if the body cannot be parsed as a Meilisearch error shape.\n174\t    /// When forwarding, the caller should use the original HTTP status code from\n175\t    /// the node response rather than deriving it from the error type.\n176\t    pub fn forwarded(json: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n177\t        #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]\n178\t        struct Raw {\n179\t            message: String,\n180\t            code: String,\n181\t            #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n182\t            error_type: raw.message,\n189\t            code: raw.code,\n190\t            error_type: raw.error_type,\n191\t            link: raw.link,\n192\t        })\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    /// Derive the HTTP status code for a miroir-generated error.\n196\t    ///\n197\t    /// For forwarded Meilisearch errors, prefer using the original node HTTP\n198\t    /// status code instead.\n199\t    pub fn http_status(&self) -> u16 {\n200\t        MiroirCode::from_code_str(&self.code)\n201\t            .map(|c| c.http_status())\n202\t            .unwrap_or_else(|| match self.error_type {\n203\t                ErrorType::InvalidRequest => 400,\n204\t                ErrorType::Auth => 401,\n205\t                ErrorType::Internal => 500,\n206\t                ErrorType::System => 503,\n207\t            })\n208\t    }\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t#[cfg(feature = \"axum\")]\n212\timpl IntoResponse for MeilisearchError {\n213\t    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n214\t        let status = .into_response()\n226\t    }\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t#[cfg(test)]\n230\tmod tests {\n231\t    use super::*;\n232\t\n233\t    // -- Per-code JSON shape tests Meilisearch-compatible API error shape and Miroir-specific error codes.\n//!\n//! All errors use the Meilisearch error shape:\n//! ```json\n//! {\"message\": \"...\", \"code\": \"...\", \"type\": \"invalid_request\", \"link\": \"...\"}\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Miroir-specific codes live under the `miroir_` prefix so that existing\n//! Meilisearch SDKs' \"unknown error\" branches handle them safely.\n\nuse serde::Serialize;\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"axum\")]\nuse axum::{http::{StatusCode, header}, response::{IntoResponse, Response}};\n\n/// Error type categories matching Meilisearch's classification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum ErrorType {\n    InvalidRequest,\n    Auth,\n    Internal,\n    System,\n}\n\n/// Miroir-specific error codes with associated metadata.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum MiroirCode {\n    PrimaryKeyRequired,\n    NoQuorum,\n    ShardUnavailable,\n    ReservedField,\n    IdempotencyKeyReused,\n    SettingsVersionStale,\n    MultiAliasNotWritable,\n    JwtInvalid,\n    JwtScopeDenied,\n    InvalidAuth,\n    MissingCsrf,\n    CsrfMismatch,\n    IndexAlreadyExists,\n    Timeout,\n}\n\nimpl MiroirCode {\n    /// All variants, used for iteration in tests.\n    #[cfg(test)]\n    const ALL: [MiroirCode; 14] = [\n        MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n        MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n        MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n        MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n        MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n        MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n        MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n        MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n        MiroirCode::IndexAlreadyExists,\n        MiroirCode::Timeout,\n    ];\n\n    /// Returns the error code string (e.g., `\"miroir_no_quorum\"`).\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::PrimaryKeyRequired => \"miroir_primary_key_required\",\n            Self::NoQuorum => \"miroir_no_quorum\",\n            Self::ShardUnavailable => \"miroir_shard_unavailable\",\n            Self::ReservedField => \"miroir_reserved_field\",\n            Self::IdempotencyKeyReused => \"miroir_idempotency_key_reused\",\n            Self::SettingsVersionStale => \"miroir_settings_version_stale\",\n            Self::MultiAliasNotWritable => \"miroir_multi_alias_not_writable\",\n            Self::JwtInvalid => \"miroir_jwt_invalid\",\n            Self::JwtScopeDenied => \"miroir_jwt_scope_denied\",\n            Self::InvalidAuth => \"miroir_invalid_auth\",\n            Self::MissingCsrf => \"miroir_missing_csrf\",\n            Self::CsrfMismatch => \"miroir_csrf_mismatch\",\n            Self::IndexAlreadyExists => \"miroir_index_already_exists\",\n            Self::Timeout => \"miroir_timeout\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the Meilisearch-compatible error type category.\n    pub fn error_type(&self) -> ErrorType {\n        match self {\n            Self::PrimaryKeyRequired\n            | Self::ReservedField\n            | Self::IdempotencyKeyReused\n            | Self::MultiAliasNotWritable\n            | Self::IndexAlreadyExists => ErrorType::InvalidRequest,\n\n            Self::JwtInvalid | Self::JwtScopeDenied | Self::InvalidAuth | Self::MissingCsrf | Self::CsrfMismatch => ErrorType::Auth,\n\n            Self::NoQuorum | Self::ShardUnavailable | Self::SettingsVersionStale | Self::Timeout => {\n                ErrorType::System\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the HTTP status code for this error.\n    pub fn http_status(&self) -> u16 {\n        match self {\n            Self::PrimaryKeyRequired | Self::ReservedField => 400,\n            Self::JwtInvalid | Self::InvalidAuth | Self::MissingCsrf => 401,\n            Self::JwtScopeDenied | Self::CsrfMismatch => 403,\n            Self::IdempotencyKeyReused | Self::MultiAliasNotWritable => 409,\n            Self::IndexAlreadyExists => 409,\n            Self::Timeout => 504,\n            Self::NoQuorum | Self::ShardUnavailable | Self::SettingsVersionStale => 503,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Generates the documentation link for this error code.\n    pub fn doc_link(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"https://github.com/jedarden/miroir/blob/main/docs/errors.md#{}\",\n            self.as_str()\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a code string back to a [`MiroirCode`].\n    pub fn from_code_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"miroir_primary_key_required\" => Some(Self::PrimaryKeyRequired),\n            \"miroir_no_quorum\" => Some(Self::NoQuorum),\n            \"miroir_shard_unavailable\" => Some(Self::ShardUnavailable),\n            \"miroir_reserved_field\" => Some(Self::ReservedField),\n            \"miroir_idempotency_key_reused\" => Some(Self::IdempotencyKeyReused),\n            \"miroir_settings_version_stale\" => Some(Self::SettingsVersionStale),\n            \"miroir_multi_alias_not_writable\" => Some(Self::MultiAliasNotWritable),\n            \"miroir_jwt_invalid\" => Some(Self::JwtInvalid),\n            \"miroir_jwt_scope_denied\" => Some(Self::JwtScopeDenied),\n            \"miroir_invalid_auth\" => Some(Self::InvalidAuth),\n            \"miroir_missing_csrf\" => Some(Self::MissingCsrf),\n            \"miroir_csrf_mismatch\" => Some(Self::CsrfMismatch),\n            \"miroir_index_already_exists\" => Some(Self::IndexAlreadyExists),\n            \"miroir_timeout\" => Some(Self::Timeout),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Meilisearch-compatible error response shape.\n///\n/// Both Miroir-specific and forwarded Meilisearch-native errors use this shape\n/// so that existing SDK error handling branches remain functional.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error, Serialize)]\n#[error(\"{message}\")]\npub struct MeilisearchError {\n    pub message: String,\n    pub code: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: ErrorType,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub link: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl MeilisearchError {\n    /// Create a new miroir-specific error with auto-generated doc link.\n    pub fn new(code: MiroirCode, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            message: message.into(),\n            code: code.as_str().to_string(),\n            error_type: code.error_type(),\n            link: Some(code.doc_link()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create from a forwarded Meilisearch node error response body.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `None` if the body cannot be parsed as a Meilisearch error shape.\n    /// When forwarding, the caller should use the original HTTP status code from\n    /// the node response rather than deriving it from the error type.\n    pub fn forwarded(json: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]\n        struct Raw {\n            message: String,\n            code: String,\n            #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n            error_type: ErrorType,\n            link: Option<String>,\n        }\n\n        let raw: Raw = serde_json::from_str(json).ok()?;\n        Some(Self {\n            message: raw.message,\n            code: raw.code,\n            error_type: raw.error_type,\n            link: raw.link,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Derive the HTTP status code for a miroir-generated error.\n    ///\n    /// For forwarded Meilisearch errors, prefer using the original node HTTP\n    /// status code instead.\n    pub fn http_status(&self) -> u16 {\n        MiroirCode::from_code_str(&self.code)\n            .map(|c| c.http_status())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| match self.error_type {\n                ErrorType::InvalidRequest => 400,\n                ErrorType::Auth => 401,\n                ErrorType::Internal => 500,\n                ErrorType::System => 503,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"axum\")]\nimpl IntoResponse for MeilisearchError {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = self.http_status();\n\n        let body = serde_json::to_string(&self).unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n            r#\"{\"message\":\"internal error\",\"code\":\"internal\",\"type\":\"internal\"}\"#.to_string()\n        });\n\n        (\n            StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n            [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n            body,\n        )\n            .into_response()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // -- Per-code JSON shape tests ------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn miroir_primary_key_required_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"primary key required for index `movies`\",\n        );\n        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"primary key required for index `movies`\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_primary_key_required\"));\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn miroir_no_quorum_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum for shard 3\",\n        );\n assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_no_quorum\"));\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 503);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn miroir_shard_unavailable_shape() {\n        let err --------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn forwarded_meilisearch_error_preserved_verbatim() {\n        let node_body = r#\"{\n            \"message\": \"Index `movies` not  }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn forwarded_meilisearch_error_without_link() {\n        let node_body = r#\"{\n            \"message\": \"bad request\",\n            \"code\": \"bad_request\",\n
