assert!(err.link.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn forwarded_invalid_json_returns_none() {\n        assert!(MeilisearchError::forwarded(\"not json\").is_none());\n        assert!(MeilisearchError::forwarded(\"{}\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn forwarded_error_serializes_back_to_same_shape() {\n        let node_body = r#\"{\n            \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n            \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n            \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n            \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#index_not_found\"\n        }\"#;\n\n        let err = MeilisearchError::forwarded(node_body).unwrap();\n        let roundtripped = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n        let original: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(node_body).unwrap();\n        let result: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&roundtripped).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(original, result);\n    }\n\n    // -- HTTP status code mapping -------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn all_codes_map_to_correct_http_status() {\n        let expected: Vec<(MiroirCode, u16)> = vec![\n            (MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired, 400),\n            (MiroirCode::ReservedField, 400),\n            (MiroirCode::JwtInvalid, 401),\n            (MiroirCode::InvalidAuth, 401),\n            (MiroirCode::MissingCsrf, 401),\n            (MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied, 403),\n            (MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch, 403),\n            (MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused, 409),\n            (MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable, 409),\n            (MiroirCode::NoQuorum, 503),\n            (MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, 503),\n            (MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale, 503),\n        ];\n\n        for (code, status) in expected {\n            assert_eq!(\n                code.http_status(),\n                status,\n                \"{:?} should map to {}\",\n                code,\n                status\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn error_http_status_matches_code_status() {\n        for variant in MiroirCode::ALL {\n            let err = MeilisearchError::new(variant, \"test\");\n            assert_eq!(\n                err.http_status(),\n                variant.http_status(),\n                \"MeilisearchError::http_status mismatch for {:?}\",\n                variant\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // -- Display impl via thiserror -----------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn display_shows_message() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, \"no quorum reached\");\n        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", err), \"no quorum reached\");\n    }\n\n    // -- Round-trip JSON serialization --------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn roundtrip_miroir_error() {\n        let original = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"shard 5 down\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&original).unwrap();\n        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(parsed[\"message\"], \"shard 5 down\");\n        assert_eq!(parsed[\"code\"], \"miroir_shard_unavailable\");\n        assert_eq!(parsed[\"type\"], \"system\");\n        assert!(parsed[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_shard_unavailable\"));\n    }\n\n    // -- Code string round-trip ---------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn code_string_roundtrip() {\n        for variant in MiroirCode::ALL {\n            let s = variant.as_str();\n            assert!(s.starts_with(\"miroir_\"), \"{} doesn't start with miroir_\", s);\n            assert_eq!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(s), Some(variant));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn from_code_str_unknown_returns_none() {\n        assert_eq!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(\"index_not_found\"), None);\n        assert_eq!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(\"\"), None);\n        assert_eq!(MiroirCode::from_code_str(\"miroir_unknown\"), None);\n    }\n\n    // -- Error type coverage: every type has at least one code ---------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn all_error_types_covered() {\n        let types: std::collections::HashSet<ErrorType> =\n            MiroirCode::ALL.iter().map(|c| c.error_type()).collect();\n        assert!(types.contains(&ErrorType::InvalidRequest));\n `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\n55\tpub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n56\t    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n57\t    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n58\t    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\timpl ProxyNodeClient {\n62\t    pub fn new(\n63\t        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n64\t        metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n65\t        replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n66\t    ) -> Self {\n67\t        Self { client, metrics, replica_selector }\n68\t    }\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\n72\timpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n73\t    async fn search_node(\n74\t        &self,\n75\t        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n76\t        address: &str,\n77\t        request: &SearchRequest,\n78\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n79\t        let start = Instant::now();\n80\t        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n81\t        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n82\t        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n83\t        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n84\t            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n85\t        }\n86\t        result\n87\t    }\n88\t\n89\t    async fn preflight_node(\n90\t        &self,\n91\t        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n92\t        address: &str,\n93\t        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n94\t    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n95\t        let start = Instant::now();\n96\t        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n97\t        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n98\t        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n99\t        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n100\t            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n101\t        }\n102\t        result\n103\t    }\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\tfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n107\t    match e {\n108\t        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n109\t        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n110\t        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n111\t    }\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\tpub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\n115\twhere\n116\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n117\t{\n118\t    axum::Router::new()\n119\t        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\t/// Search request body.\n123\t#[derive(Deserialize, Serialize)]\n124\tstruct SearchRequestBody {\n125\t    q: Option<String>,\n126\t    offset: Option<usize>,\n127\t    limit: Option<usize>,\n128\t    filter: Option<Value>,\n129\t    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n130\t    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n131\t    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n132\t    #[serde(flatten)]\n133\t    rest: Value,\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\timpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n137\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n138\t        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n139\t            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n140\t            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n141\t            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n142\t            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n143\t            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n144\t            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n145\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n146\t    }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n150\t///\n151\t/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n152\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n153\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n154\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n155\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n156\t///\n157\t/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n158\t/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n159\t/// explicitly requested it.\n160\t///\n161\t/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n162\t/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n163\t#[tracing::instrument(skip(state, headers, body))]\n164\tasync fn search_handler(\n165\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n166\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n167\t    OptionalSessionId(session_id): OptionalSessionId,\n168\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n169\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n170\t) -> Response<Body> {\n171\t    let start = Instant::now();\n172\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n173\t\n174\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n175\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|s| {\n176\t        if s.as_str().is_empty() { None } else { Some(s) }\n177\t    });\n178\t\n179\t    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers - need to add back HeaderMap extraction\n180\t    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n181\t\n182\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n183\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n184\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n185\t        Err(e) => {\n186\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n187\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n188\t        }\n189\t    };\n190\t\n191\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n192\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n193\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n194\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n195\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n196\t                    if !allowed {\n197\t                        warn!(\n198\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n199\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n200\t                        );\n201\t                        return Response::builder()\n202\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n203\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n204\t                .unwrap();\n205\t                    }\n206\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n207\t                }\n208\t                Err(e) => {\n209\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n210\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n211\t                }\n212\t            }\n213\t        }\n214\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n215\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n216\t            &source_ip,\n217\t            limit,\n218\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n219\t        );\n220\t        if !allowed {\n221\t            warn!(\n222\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n223\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n224\t            );\n225\t            return Response::builder()\n226\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n227\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n228\t                .unwrap();\n229\t        }\n230\t    }\n231\t\n232\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n233\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n234\t    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n235\t        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n236\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n237\t        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n238\t            // It's an alias\n239\t            (index.clone(), targets)\n240\t        } else {\n241\t            // Not an alias - use index directly\n242\t            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n243\t        }\n244\t    } else {\n245\t        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n246\t        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n247\t    };\n248\t\n249\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n250\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n251\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n252\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n253\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n254\t            match strategy {\n255\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n256\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n257\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n258\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n259\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n260\t                        sid.as_str(),\n261\t                        &state.task_registry,\n262\t                        max_wait,\n263\t                    ).await {\n264\t                        Ok(true) => {\n265\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n266\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n267\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n268\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n269\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n270\t                        }\n271\t                        Ok(false) => {\n272\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n273\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n274\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n275\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n276\t                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n277\t                        }\n278\t                        Err(_) => {\n279\t                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n280\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n281\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n282\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n283\t                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n284\t                        }\n285\t                    }\n286\t                }\n287\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n288\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n289\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n290\t                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n291\t                }\n292\t            }\n293\t        } else {\n294\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n295\t            (None, None)\n296\t        }\n297\t    } else {\n298\t        // No session header - normal routing\n299\t        (None, None)\n300\t    };\n301\t\n302\t    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n303\t    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n304\t        debug!(\n305\t            session_id = ?sid,\n306\t            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n307\t            strategy = label,\n308\t            \"session pinning applied\"\n309\t        );\n310\t    }\n311\t\n312\t    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n313\t    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n314\t    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n315\t        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n316\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n317\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n318\t        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n319\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n320\t            query_json,\n321\t            settings_version,\n322\t        };\n323\t\n324\t        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n325\t        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n326\t            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n327\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n328\t            debug!(\n329\t                index = %effective_index,\n330\t                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n331\t            );\n332\t\n333\t            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n334\t            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n335\t            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n336\t                rx.recv().await\n337\t            }).await;\n338\t\n339\t            match response_bytes {\n340\t                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n341\t                    // Received response from coalesced query\n342\t                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n343\t                        Ok(v) => v,\n344\t                        Err(e) => {\n345\t                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n346\t                            return Response::builder()\n347\t                                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n348\t                                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n349\t                                .unwrap();\n350\t                        }\n351\t                    };\n352\t\n353\t                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n354\t                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n355\t                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n356\t                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n357\t\n358\t                    // Add settings headers\n359\t                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n360\t                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n361\t                    }\n362\t                    if settings_version > 0 {\n363\t                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n364\t                    }\n365\t\n366\t                    let response = response\n367\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n368\t                        .unwrap();\n369\t\n370\t                    tracing::info!(\n371\t                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n372\t                        index = %effective_index,\n373\t                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n374\t                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n375\t                    );\n376\t\n377\t                    return response;\n378\t                }\n379\t                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n380\t                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n381\t                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n382\t                }\n383\t                Err(_) => {\n384\t                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n385\t                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n386\t                }\n387\t            }\n388\t        }\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n392\t    // Extract early for multi-target search path\n393\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n394\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n395\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n396\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n397\t\n398\t    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n399\t    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n400\t    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n401\t    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n402\t        // Need to create a new Extension wrapper for the nested call\n403\t        // Clone the Arc for the multi-target search\n404\t        return search_multi_targets(\n405\t            resolved_targets,\n406\t            body,\n407\t            Extension(state.clone()),\n408\t            sid.map(|s| s.0),\n409\t            client_requested_score,\n410\t            min_settings_version,\n411\t        ).await;\n412\t    }\n413\t\n414\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n415\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n416\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n417\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n418\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n419\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n420\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n421\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n422\t                &state.pod_id,\n423\t                &index,\n424\t                sk.generation,\n425\t            );\n426\t\n427\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n428\t            sk.primary_key\n429\t        } else {\n430\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n431\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n432\t        }\n433\t    } else {\n434\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n435\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n436\t    };\n437\t\n438\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n439\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n440\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n441\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n442\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n443\t\n444\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n445\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n446\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n447\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n448\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n449\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n450\t        _ => return Response::builder()\n451\t            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n452\t            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n453\t            .unwrap(),\n454\t    };\n455\t\n456\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n457\t    let plan = {\n458\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n459\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n460\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n461\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n462\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n463\t            min_settings_version,\n464\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n465\t            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n466\t        ).entered();\n467\t\n468\t        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n469\t        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n470\t        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n471\t            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n472\t        } else {\n473\t            None\n474\t        };\n475\t\n476\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n477\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n478\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n479\t                &topo,\n480\t                0,\n481\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n482\t                state.config.shards,\n483\t                group,\n484\t                replica_selector_ref,\n485\t            ).await {\n486\t                Some(p) => p,\n487\t                None => {\n488\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n489\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n490\t                    warn!(\n491\t                        pinned_group = group,\n492\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n493\t                    );\n494\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n495\t                }\n496\t            }\n497\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n498\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n499\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n500\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n501\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n502\t                &topo,\n503\t                0,\n504\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n505\t                state.config.shards,\n506\t                &index,\n507\t                floor,\n508\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n509\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n510\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n511\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n512\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n513\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n514\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n515\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n516\t                        })\n517\t                    })\n518\t                },\n519\t                replica_selector_ref,\n520\t            ).await;\n521\t\n522\t            match plan_result {\n523\t                Some(p) => p,\n524\t                None => {\n525\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n526\t                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n527\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n528\t                        format!(\n529\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n530\t                            floor, index\n531\t                        ),\n532\t                    );\n533\t                    return Response::builder()\n534\t                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n535\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n536\t                        .unwrap();\n537\t                }\n538\t            }\n539\t        } else {\n540\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n541\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n542\t        }\n543\t    };\n544\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n545\t\n546\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n547\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n548\t\n549\t    // Build search request\n550\t    // Clone facets for fingerprinting before moving into SearchRequest\n551\t    let facets_clone = body.facets.clone();\n552\t    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n553\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n554\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n555\t        query: body.q,\n556\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n557\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n558\t        filter: body.filter,\n559\t        facets: body.facets,\n560\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n561\t        body: rest_body,\n562\t        global_idf: None,\n563\t    };\n564\t\n565\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n566\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n567\t        search_key,\n568\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n569\t    ));\n570\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n571\t\n572\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n573\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n574\t\n575\t    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n576\t    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n577\t    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n578\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n579\t        // Reconstruct body for fingerprinting (use cloned facets)\n580\t        let fingerprint_body = SearchRequestBody {\n581\t            q: search_req.query.clone(),\n582\t            offset: Some(search_req.offset),\n583\t            limit: Some(search_req.limit),\n584\t            filter: search_req.filter.clone(),\n585\t             // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n603\t                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n604\t .await\n621\t    {\n622\t        Ok(r) => r,\n623\t        Err(e) => {\n624\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n625\t            return Response::builder()\n626\t Response};\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::Json;\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::QueryFingerprint;\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::replica_selection::SelectionObserver;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, plan_search_scatter_for_group, plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse miroir_core::session_pinning::WaitStrategy;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, warn};\n\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::{AppState, parse_rate_limit};\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\n\n/// Metrics observer for replica selection events.\n///\n/// Reports selection scores and exploration events to Prometheus.\nstruct MetricsObserver {\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl SelectionObserver for MetricsObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64) {\n        self.metrics.set_replica_selection_score(node_id, score);\n    }\n\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {\n        self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(\n        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n        metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n        replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics, replica_selector }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize, Serialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[tracing::instrument(skip(state, headers, body))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    OptionalSessionId(session_id): OptionalSessionId,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Response<Body> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|s| {\n        if s.as_str().is_empty() { None } else { Some(s) }\n    });\n\n    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers - need to add back HeaderMap extraction\n    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid.as_str(),\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n            );\n\n            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n                rx.recv().await\n            }).await;\n\n            match response_bytes {\n                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n                    // Received response from coalesced query\n                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n                        Ok(v) => v,\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n                            return Response::builder()\n                                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                                .unwrap();\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n                    // Add settings headers\n                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n                    }\n                    if settings_version > 0 {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n                    }\n\n                    let response = response\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n                        .unwrap();\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n                        index = %effective_index,\n                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n                    );\n\n                    return response;\n                }\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    // Extract early for multi-target search path\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        // Need to create a new Extension wrapper for the nested call\n        // Clone the Arc for the multi-target search\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            Extension(state.clone()),\n            sid.map(|s| s.0),\n            client_requested_score,\n            min_settings_version,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Response::builder()\n            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n            .unwrap(),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                        .unwrap();\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    // Clone facets for fingerprinting before moving into SearchRequest\n    let facets_clone = body.facets.clone();\n    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: rest_body,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        // Reconstruct body for fingerprinting (use cloned facets)\n        let fingerprint_body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: search_req.query.clone(),\n            offset: Some(search_req.offset),\n            limit: Some(search_req.limit),\n            filter: search_req.filter.clone(),\n            facets: facets_clone,\n            ranking_score: Some(search_req.ranking_score),\n            rest: search_req.body.clone(),\n        };\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&fingerprint_body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n                (None, None)\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    {\n        Ok(r) => r,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n            );\n        }\n        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n    min_settings_version: Option<u64>,\n) -> Response<Body> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers\n    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = session_id {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid.as_str(),\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = match targets.first() {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => {\n            error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Response::builder()\n            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n            .unwrap(),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n    // should have identical shard layout)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n            primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                None,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                None,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, primary_target\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                        .unwrap();\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n        query: body.q.clone(),\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    {\n        Ok(r) => r,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add settings headers\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n        primary_target = %primary_target,\n        target_count = targets.len(),\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"multi-target search completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\npub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        if !client_requested_score {\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private data\".to_string()),\n            offset: Some(0),\n            limit: Some(20),\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!({\"email\": \"user@example.com\"})),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".to_string()]),\n            ranking_score: Some(false),\n            rest: serde_json::json!({}),\n        };\n        let assert!(\n            !debug_output.contains(\"user@example.com\"),\n            \"Debug output should not contain filter values\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n {\n105\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n106\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 idempotency_key).await\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n176\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n177\tasync fn put_documents(\n178\t
